question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AtomicLong lastConsensusTimestamp in SharedPollingTopicListener.PollingContext to cause a network partition: if the DB returns messages with a very large consensusTimestamp during partition recovery, lastConsensusTimestamp is set to a future value, causing all subsequent polls to return empty results and silently drop messages for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval).maxBackoff(interval.multipliedBy(4L)) in SharedPollingTopicListener to cause a network partition: during a DB partition, the retry backoff never exceeds 4x the interval, causing rapid retry storms that prevent the DB from recovering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnCancel(() -> log.info('Cancelled polling')) in SharedPollingTopicListener to cause a network partition: by repeatedly subscribing and cancelling, the shared Flux is repeatedly cancelled and recreated, causing a window where no polling occurs and messages are lost for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Schedulers.boundedElastic() in PollingTopicListener to cause a network partition: by opening many subscriptions each with their own scheduler, the bounded elastic thread pool is exhausted, causing new subscriptions to queue indefinitely and effectively partitioning new subscribers from the service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the delaySubscription(interval, scheduler) in PollingTopicListener.listen() to cause a network partition: by opening many subscriptions simultaneously, the delayed subscriptions all fire at the same time after the interval, causing a thundering herd of DB queries that partitions the DB?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter.hasLimit() check in PollingTopicListener.poll() to cause a network partition: by setting limit=0 (which hasLimit() returns false for), the poll issues queries with Integer.MAX_VALUE as the limit, causing the DB to attempt to return billions of rows and partitioning it from other services?
